parsed_review.summary:
CP00-564 is a clean descriptor-only AI Legal Workflows (RP18) closeout pack covering 150 units (RP18.P04.M06.S03–RP18.P05.M02.S14, risk C). Independent read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob confirms the pack-level claims are supported and no boundaries are violated.

Scope integrity: pack binding (registry.js:222), CP564 requirements (registry.js:1300), manifest, contract projection (contract line 14774), and baseline agree on unit_count=150, range, risk_class=C, upstream=CP00-563, next=CP00-565, next_subphase=RP18.P05.M02.S15. Deliverable distribution (ui:43, claude_review:13, security_audit:14, implementation:41, fixture:25, test:8, hermes_evidence:6) and micro-title distribution match the baseline exactly and sum to 150; manifest contains exactly 150 included units; phase split P04:106 / P05:44 verified.

Production-ready posture is honest and supported: manifest declares production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review (manifest.json:5-9), matching command-evidence.json (production_ready=false, no_real_data=true, writes_product_state=false; all listed commands exit 0). The only readiness signal is production_ready_candidate at the Hermes evidence level (validators.js:2581), which is descriptor-scoped and does not assert enterprise trust.

No-write/no-real-data: freezeCp564Result (service.js:1677) sets all runtime/write/database/object-storage/audit/hermes-runtime flags false and synthetic_only/no_real_data true; enforced by validators (validators.js:2540-2547) and tests (model.test.js:969-970). service.js imports only descriptor constants from registry.js — no fs/fetch/child_process/db/runtime I/O.

Authority boundary: claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, read_only=true across descriptor, attestation, contract, tests, and the validator script (validate-rp18-...mjs:773-776). Hermes is confined to deterministic evidence/receipt handling (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false; receipt_shape '...no_write'); hermes_runtime_embedded_in_product=false.

Review hardening (CP178+): readiness auth preflight passed with valid 1444-byte JSON (exit 0); forbidden_review_evidence list (failed auth, zero-byte, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, missing fields, final-approval/enterprise-trust claims) is embedded and asserted; invalid_attempts_preserved is empty. Changed-file scope correctly isolates the 8 active RP18 implementation refs and classifies unrelated dirty files (progress-control-room, LDIP invalid-attempt, workbook docs) as preserved/non-pack.

Post-review evidence artifacts referenced by the manifest (claude-review-result.json, adjudication.md, construction-inspection.json) are not yet present; this is the expected gating sequence — they are produced from this review and run before commit — not a defect. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings. Final enterprise-trust acceptance remains human-gated, consistent with the authority boundary.
